codeReview.summary: Fail: the Android module covers the requested registration, manifest, and dependency wiring, but the secret-sealing path combines `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)` with a caller-supplied GCM IV during `Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE`. On Android Keystore AES/GCM keys this can reject provisioning at runtime, so first-launch vault creation is not reliable.

issues.description: The key spec enables `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)` at lines 117-122, but `generateAndStoreSecret()` still generates its own IV and passes it into `cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key, GCMParameterSpec(...))` at lines 259-264. For Android Keystore AES/GCM keys, randomized-encryption-required mode rejects caller-provided IVs on encryption, so provisioning can fail with a `GeneralSecurityException` instead of storing the wallet secret. Initialize the cipher without an IV (`cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key)`) and then persist the provider-generated `cipher.iv`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature's mission artifacts are incomplete: `features.json` marks the feature completed, but there is no completed worker session, no handoff file for the assigned worker session, and no transcript skeleton entry to review. That breaks the `mobile-feature-worker` / mission handoff trail and makes it impossible to audit how CI verification was performed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` lines 995-1000 show `status: "completed"` with `workerSessionIds: ["df61d8ff-e408-48d6-9eae-9cffeb26f33d"]` but `completedWorkerSessionId: null`; `progress_log.jsonl` lines 38-40 show that same worker session only started and then failed from daemon inactivity; `AGENTS.md` lines 106-112 require every worker to produce a handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still documents the wrong canonical biometric error-code union. `AGENTS.md` lists `TIMEOUT`, while the shipped spec/tests/native implementations use `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT` instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `AGENTS.md` line 52 lists `(USER_CANCELED, BIOMETRY_UNAVAILABLE, BIOMETRY_NOT_ENROLLED, BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT, KEY_INVALIDATED, NOT_FOUND, AUTH_FAILED, VAULT_ERROR, TIMEOUT)`, but `src/lib/enbox/__tests__/native-biometric-vault.test.ts` lines 45-54 define the nine canonical codes with `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT` and no `TIMEOUT`; the iOS and Android native vault files use that same set.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation contract's Android IV guidance is too permissive for Keystore-backed AES/GCM. It says either a provider-generated IV or a caller-generated `GCMParameterSpec` IV is acceptable, but once `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)` is also required, the caller-supplied-IV path leads directly to the runtime failure in this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md` lines 3388-3395 allow both `cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key)` and a caller-generated `GCMParameterSpec(128, iv)` while separately requiring `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)`; `NativeBiometricVaultModule.kt` lines 120 and 259-264 follow that combination.

summary: Fail: the reviewed diff wires up the Android NativeBiometricVault module, manifest permissions, dependency, and TurboModule registration, but `generateAndStoreSecret()` is not safe to ship because it supplies its own GCM IV while the Keystore key requires randomized encryption. I also could not review a transcript skeleton or handoff for the assigned worker session because the mission metadata only records a timed-out worker and no completion artifacts.
